characters.physical_details: Lean, dark-haired, sun-weathered, rough-handed, and easy to overlook. Unwritability has no visible marker. Later chapters add a healing forearm cut, post-consumption tremors, and lingering Woundmouth strain.

key_relationships.aldric: dead paladin fragment in his head from Chapter 1; unwilling guide who chose him deliberately
key_relationships.vess: dead Nightguild assassin fragment from Chapter 4 to Chapter 16; tactical foil who leaves to stabilize him
key_relationships.derry: oldest friend and fraud partner for eleven years; leaves in Chapter 13 after confirming the Crook direction to Caul
key_relationships.thessa: real sin-eater who exposes his fraud in Silt, nearly has him consumed, then trains him at the Crook
key_relationships.caul: hero-ranger hunting the bounty; his accusation forces Maren to face the human cost of fake clearings

characters.occupation_status: Fraudulent public sin-eater in Fenhold for eleven years; the System showed an active level-seven sin-eater profile over an unwritable, hollow architecture. Reclassified as an unclassified entity in Chapter 3; by Chapter 18 he is doing genuine clearing work.
characters.literacy_languages: Literate in common text. Cannot see System text; relied on Derry for interface prompts until Aldric began reading them aloud.

state_transitions.change: Accidentally performs a real opening over Haff Garsen and takes in Aldric's fragment, combat training, and grief.
System reclassifies him as an unclassified entity and broadcasts a 4,000-gold-crown bounty; anonymity ends.
Failed hollow consumption pulls in Vess; opposed fragments create the unstable unclassified entity.
Kills a bounty hunter with borrowed combat instincts and suffers first-combat shock plus a forearm cut.
Performs his first partial genuine consumption; clears part of a pool and pays with vomiting, tremors, and collapse.
Overloads on Crook contamination, cracks the bowl, widens his aperture, and triggers local System reclassification.
Expels Vess through the bowl; the entity simplifies and his headache and shakes begin to ease.
At the Woundmouth, briefly sees the interface, understands the System as containment, and partially seals a structural fracture.
Completes his first routine paying job as a real sin-eater at Barren Rill.

characters.physical_details: Dead paladin fragment with no body of his own. In life he was broad and disciplined; Maren carries flashes of those habits. Died at about thirty-five.

key_relationships.maren: chosen System-unwritable host; investigation subject turned partner
key_relationships.vess: opposed fragment in the same host until Chapter 16
key_relationships.order_of_the_veil: former order; source of his training, authority, and doomed anomaly work

characters.occupation_status: Aldric Venn, knight-paladin of the Order of the Veil's Fourth Seal and later senior anomaly investigator. Died in a dungeon whose rules appear to have been rewritten mid-survey.
characters.literacy_languages: Fully literate in common, religious, and technical System-reading contexts; can read public interface and deeper architectural layers.

state_transitions.change: Embeds in Maren instead of dissolving and begins reading the interface for him.
Sees the containment architecture flex during Maren's real consumption and falls into a two-day silence.
Reveals that Maren is unwritable, that the System is containment, and that he chose Maren deliberately.
Becomes the only remaining fragment after Vess's separation.
Keeps Maren alive through the Woundmouth sealing and drops much of his old report-formality afterward.

characters.physical_details: Dead Nightguild assassin fragment. In life she was small, sharp, and unobtrusive; in death some memories, feelings, and names are cleanly excised rather than blurred.

key_relationships.maren: host she never chose; later leaves to stop destabilizing him
key_relationships.aldric: opposed paladin fragment sharing the same host until Chapter 16
key_relationships.torren_blackhall: unfinished contract target; minor lord who hid a massacre under quest noise

characters.occupation_status: Nightguild assassin killed mid-contract inside Blackhall's quest zone; persists as a fragment from Chapter 4 until Maren separates her in Chapter 16.
characters.literacy_languages: Literate in common and guild codes; reads tactical/System threat data and bodies in motion.

state_transitions.change: Arrives through Maren's failed containment and immediately identifies the host/paladin situation as a disaster.
Her Blackhall contract, missing memory interval, missing sister's name, and emotional excisions become explicit.
Talks Maren through post-consumption recovery with unexpected steadiness.
Chooses separation, releases the Blackhall contract, and dissipates as an unhosted fragment.

characters.availability: Internal voice from Chapter 4 through Chapter 16 only.

characters.physical_details: Stocky farmer with strong hands and an open face. Stress shows physically: sweat, strap-fidgeting, and later a deliberate walking distance from Maren.

key_relationships.maren: oldest friend and fraud partner for eleven years
key_relationships.lenna: wife in Fenhold; Derry's pull back toward home
key_relationships.pell: daughter, not yet three at story start and turning three during the journey; the reason he leaves

characters.occupation_status: Fenhold farmer who helped Maren fake rites by reading System cues and sharing the fee. Leaves the road in Chapter 13 to return to Lenna and Pell.
characters.literacy_languages: Functionally literate; can read ordinary text and interface prompts well enough to feed Maren cues.

state_transitions.change: Leaves Fenhold with Maren after the bounty appears, separating from Lenna and Pell.
Caul's account of Haley and Renn makes the fraud's cost personal again; Derry's trust breaks further.
Admits he confirmed the Crook direction to Caul, accepts safe passage home, and leaves before dawn.

characters.availability: Present in Chapters 1-13; absent in Chapters 14-18.

characters.physical_details: Tall, angular, grey-haired, with permanently stained hands and visible contamination wear from three decades of real work.

key_relationships.maren: first exposes him as a fraud, then trains him because his aperture can reduce contamination she cannot

characters.occupation_status: Real sin-eater based around Silt; about thirty years on the eastern routes, Crook veteran, and one of too few practitioners left active in her region.
characters.literacy_languages: Fully literate in ritual procedure, contamination assessment, and System-maintenance practice.

state_transitions.change: Confirms Maren is a fraud, lists named damage in Dunmore, Cress, and Fenhold, and refuses to teach him in Silt.
At the Crook, argues for consuming him, supervises a test, sees valley-scale reduction, and begins harsh training.
Loses Petra, Ruald, and Denna during the Crook correction and escapes with Kael and Hale.
Acknowledges Maren as a working sin-eater and assigns him Barren Rill plus the next site.

characters.availability: Present in Chapters 7 and 14-18; referenced elsewhere.

characters.physical_details: Weathered ranger with repaired gear, short-cropped hair, old smile lines, and a bad right knee.

key_relationships.haley: dead healer; Caul carries her bird drawings and the memory of her contamination death
key_relationships.renn: dead shield-bearer from the same failed 'cleared' site

characters.occupation_status: Caul Henning of the Wayward Reach ranger company; twelve years active service. Picks up Maren's bounty in Harrow while already tracking fake-sin-eater cases after Haley Keth and Renn Ostara die at an uncleared site.
characters.literacy_languages: Fully literate in field reports, tracking, interface scanning, and bounty procedure.

state_transitions.change: Confronts Maren, explains Haley and Renn's deaths, and gives him one day to reach the Crook for proper judgment.
Arrives with a four-person hero party, loses party members to the Crook reclassification, and abandons pursuit to hold the ridge for the surviving boy.

characters.availability: Present in Chapters 10 and 15; referenced as the active hunter in Chapters 3, 9, and 13.

timeline.story_start: Late summer harvest season in Fenhold, when uncleared death-contamination threatens fields, water, and grain stores.
timeline.estimated_span: About four weeks from Haff Garsen's ritual to Maren's first paid legitimate clearing at Barren Rill.

seasonal_track.season: Late summer: hot roads, dust, harvest labor, and contamination ripening in the heat.
Summer turning: cooler mornings, first mist, and early yellow in the trees.
Early autumn: cold nights, sharper air, and recently harvested fields.

key_events.event: Maren's fake rite over Haff Garsen becomes real and Aldric embeds instead of dissolving.
The System reclassifies Maren as an unclassified entity and broadcasts a 4,000-gold-crown bounty; he and Derry flee Fenhold.
Failed hollow consumption pulls in Vess and creates the unstable unclassified entity.
Maren kills his first bounty hunter on the road.
Thessa in Silt exposes the eleven-year fraud and its named contamination damage.
In Harrow, Maren's blank profile is identified publicly; quiet travel through towns becomes impossible.
Caul explains Haley and Renn's deaths at an uncleared site and gives Maren one day to reach the Crook.
Maren performs his first partial real consumption and Aldric begins seeing the containment architecture directly.
Aldric reveals that Maren is unwritable, the System is containment, and he chose Maren deliberately.
Derry tells Caul the Crook direction and leaves before dawn.
The Crook examines Maren, nearly decides to consume him, then Thessa starts training him after a successful supervised reduction.
Caul's party attacks the Crook; Petra, Ruald, and Denna die; the System reclassifies the valley as a critical breach site.
Vess separates and dissipates, stabilizing Maren's remaining unclassified state.
At the Woundmouth, Maren briefly sees the interface, understands the System as containment, and partially seals a structural fracture.
Maren clears Barren Rill for pay, beginning ordinary real sin-eater work.

geography.primary_setting: Frontier region where villages, roads, and hero towns sit atop quest infrastructure, contamination runoff, and dungeon mouths.

key_locations.name: Fenhold
key_locations.details: Poor farming village on the Fenhold flats. Maren's home and fraud territory for eleven years; contamination has been building there since he was seventeen.

key_locations.name: Silt
key_locations.details: Market town about six days south of Fenhold and Thessa's working base; practical trade town rather than a hero staging center.

key_locations.name: Blackhall holdings
key_locations.details: Minor noble estate east of the Silt road. Torren Blackhall purchased quest access there and used quest noise to cover a massacre in the tenant villages.

key_locations.name: Harrow
key_locations.details: Hero town dense with quest boards, gear shops, passive scans, damage leaderboards, and casual scorch marks; Maren's blank profile is publicly confirmed here.

key_locations.name: The Crook
key_locations.details: Small contamination camp in a valley between three dungeon mouths. Only five active sin-eaters remain when Maren arrives, though fourteen designations once served the site.

key_locations.name: The Woundmouth
key_locations.details: An eight-foot tear in the rock where quest-light bleeds instead of pooling and the containment architecture is thinnest.

key_locations.name: Barren Rill
key_locations.details: Small farm settlement by a dead creek. Their sin-eater Dalla died in spring, leaving nine months of contamination behind the house and in the well-water table.

distances: Fenhold to Silt: about six days on foot by the southern road.
Silt to Harrow: several days east/northeast into heavier hero traffic and denser System saturation.
Harrow to the Crook: about four days north, or five if the road has deteriorated past dungeon-approach standards.
Crook to the Woundmouth: three days east after the roads give out.
Chapter 18 posting to Barren Rill: about twelve miles east from Thessa's roadside meeting point.

world_rules: Most people are born into a visible System interface; Maren is rarer than ordinary System-blindness because the System cannot write on him.
A public profile can exist over hollow or damaged deeper architecture; before Chapter 3 Maren publicly read as an active level-seven sin-eater even though the underlying record was blank.
Quest-light is a physical, faintly warm luminescence visible even to Maren; System-sighted people see data layered over it.
Scorch marks are lasting residue from hero-class abilities and are usually left in place, especially in hero towns.
Landholders can buy quest generation or quest access on their territory; Blackhall uses that infrastructure to hide murder.
Contamination is graded as active, residual, or structural. Structural contamination can become part of soil, water, and foundations and often cannot be identified by smell alone.
Real sin-eating requires controlled openness, proper intake, and dissolution. If contamination does not seat first, a bad consumer can redistribute it across a wider area instead of clearing it.
Real practitioners use different bowls or vessels for active work, residual work, and structural assessment.
Fragments are dead consciousnesses embedded in uncleared contamination. In Maren's unwritable host-mind they persist as voices instead of dissolving.
Opposed fragments inside Maren created the unclassified entity. Removing one fragment stabilizes it but does not make him classifiable.

The System treats unclassified entities as repair problems: it escalates from bounty broadcasts to local breach-site reclassification and spawned correction creatures that target classified entities in the zone.
The System is a built containment wall holding something older and less classifiable on the far side. Sin-eaters are maintenance workers repairing leaks in that wall.

power_structure.over: All classified people, places, objects, and breaches
power_structure.mechanism: Writes public profiles, assigns quests, posts bounties, and issues automated corrections when classifications fail.

power_structure.over: Quest execution, bounty pursuit, and local force projection
power_structure.mechanism: They read the System's postings and turn them into organized parties, patrols, and hunting work.
Low-status maintenance specialists who clear residue, assess layering, and keep settlements livable; too few remain active.
Paladin order that studied deeper architecture and suspected the System was containment; Aldric's work suggests the order pushed too close to suppressed knowledge.

power_structure.over: Villages, estates, and local quest economies
power_structure.mechanism: Territorial authority plus the ability to buy quest access or profit from hero traffic, as Torren Blackhall does.

objects.name: Maren's ceramic bowl
objects.origin: Cheap Fenhold market bowl bought at seventeen for four coppers and used for eleven years as a prop before it ever did real work.
objects.status: Used in every fake rite; works as a real focus by Chapter 11; cracked during the Crook overload in Chapter 15 but still functions for Vess's separation, the Woundmouth, and Barren Rill.

objects.name: Aldric's veil-sigil
objects.origin: Order of the Veil insignia carried in Aldric's surviving memories.
objects.status: Not physically present; recurs only through Maren's inherited memories.

income_sources.amount: About 12 coppers per rite; Maren later reckons the whole eleven-year fraud at roughly 1,500 coppers, with fees usually split with Derry and meals often included
income_sources.reliability: Steady until the Chapter 3 flight, but based entirely on fraud.

income_sources.source: Legitimate clearing fees after the Woundmouth
income_sources.amount: Ad hoc; Barren Rill pays 6 coppers and a meal for a mid-grade clearing
income_sources.reliability: Begins in Chapter 18; real work exists, but pay is low and irregular.

knowledge_state.knows: He faked sin-eating for eleven years; Aldric and later Vess are in his head; by Chapter 8 he knows their missing memories look edited rather than decayed; by Chapter 12 he knows he is unwritable to the System, Aldric chose him, and the System is containment; by Chapter 17 he knows he can reach and seal structural cracks.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Mostly everyone outside his head; Derry leaves before learning the containment truth, and Thessa never hears the full wall explanation on-page.

knowledge_state.knows: Maren's fraud, the fact that he supplied System cues, the scale of the contamination harm once Thessa and Caul spell it out, and the Crook direction he gives Caul.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Lenna, Pell, and Fenhold before the bounty breaks the lie open.

knowledge_state.knows: Maren is not merely blind but unwritable, the System is a containment wall, his own dungeon death may have been a targeted correction, and he chose Maren on purpose.
knowledge_state.learned_in: Pre-death investigation; chapter 1 onward; revealed aloud in chapter 12

knowledge_state.knows: Torren Blackhall used purchased quest noise to hide a massacre, her own contract details, and that key memories and feelings have been selectively excised, including her sister's name.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Maren until Chapters 8 and 16; everyone else throughout.

knowledge_state.knows: Maren's fraud spread contamination wider than standard channels could catch, and his widened aperture can reduce connected fields on a scale normal sin-eaters cannot.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Most settlements they serve; she does not publicize his capabilities.

knowledge_state.knows: Haley Keth and Renn Ostara died because a supposedly cleared site still held deep contamination, and Maren's blank profile matches the broader fake-sin-eater problem he has been tracking.
knowledge_state.learned_in: Three months pre-story; confirmed in chapters 9-10
knowledge_state.hidden_from: He withholds the one-day head-start arrangement from other heroes on-page.

environmental_constants: Quest-light is visible raw glow to everyone and feels faintly warm; in towns it runs through gutters and ditches like luminous water.
Scorch marks from hero abilities linger on roads, walls, and buildings for years and are usually ignored rather than repaired.
High System density produces a hum that Maren hears physically in teeth, bones, and sinuses; it intensifies near dungeon mouths, heavy residue, and breach sites.
Contamination smell shifts with age: fresh active contamination is ozone-sweet, residual contamination drier and sourer, and deeply structural contamination can lose smell entirely.
Dungeon mouths physically alter nearby ground and air; around the Woundmouth, quest-light bleeds from stone instead of pooling.
Roads worsen the deeper one goes into dungeon territory, from maintained ruts to hero-scarred tracks to no true road at all.
